title: The Web Application Hacker's Handbook
subtitle: Finding and Exploiting Security Flaws (2nd edition)
author: Dafydd Stuttard and Marcus Pinto

source_book.title: The Web Application Hacker's Handbook: Finding and Exploiting Security Flaws (2nd edition)

audience.self_description: pentesters, appsec engineers, security-minded developers, bug bounty hunters

value_proposition.before: Security assessments rely on running automated scanners and checking generic vulnerability lists. Code reviews miss subtle injection patterns and logic flaws.
value_proposition.after: Systematic, methodology-driven security assessments that follow structured test procedures for each vulnerability category with specific findings and targeted remediation.
value_proposition.key_differentiator: Structured per-vulnerability-class detection methodologies with specific test steps, filter bypass techniques, language-specific dangerous API catalogs, and the comprehensive 13-area testing methodology.
